title: drymaria divaricata var. divaricata
intro: Drymaria divaricata var. divaricata, also known as tumbleweed or creeping spilanthus, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Madderaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. It has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, colds, and skin infections, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
